babyDevelopment: The fetus is developing reflexes. If the abdomen is touched, the baby will squirm (though you still can't feel it). The kidneys are producing urine, which is released into the amniotic sac. The brain's nerve cells are multiplying rapidly.
bodyChanges: The risk of miscarriage drops significantly after this week. Your uterus is moving out of your pelvis and into your abdomen, which might reduce bladder pressure slightly. You may start to feel more energetic.
tips: Plan your 'babymoon' or travel during the second trimester
Share the news with friends and family if you haven't yet
Maintain a balanced diet with plenty of nutrients

do: Eat small, frequent meals if nausea persists
Focus on good posture as your center of gravity shifts
dont: Ignore your body's signals for rest

travelAdvisory.note: Most airlines allow travel up to week 36, but check specific rules.

milestoneDescription: Reflexes are beginning to form as the brain and muscles connect.
